quoted_tweet.text:
<구글 딥마인드와 함께, 대한민국 AI 혁신의 새로운 길을 열어갑니다>

오늘 구글 딥마인드의 데미스 하사비스(@demishassabis) CEO와 만나 AI 협력에 관한 MoU를 체결했습니다. AI 발전 방향에 대해 논의한 대통령 접견에 이어, 과학기술 분야에서의 협력을 구체화하는 계기로 10년전 알파고 대결이 펼쳐졌던 포시즌즈 호텔에서 진행되었습니다.

10년 전 알파고가 AI 시대의 시작을 알렸고 10년이 지난 지금, AI는 예상보다 훨씬 빠르게 발전하며 과학과 산업 전반에 큰 변화를 만들어내고 있습니다. 알파폴드와 같은 사례는 AI가 과학적 발견의 속도를 바꾸고 있다는 점을 잘 보여줍니다.

저 역시 산업 현장에서 소재와 바이오 분야에 AI를 적용하는 과정을 경험하며, 과학 분야에서 AI를 통한 혁신은 반드시 가능하다고 느껴왔습니다. 이제는 우리가 그 변화를 어떻게 준비할 것인지가 더 중요해졌습니다.

오늘 하사비스 CEO와의 대화에서도 우리가 또 다른 전환점에 서 있다는 점을 확인할 수 있었습니다. 앞으로 더 높은 수준의 AI 기술이 빠른 속도로 확산될 가능성은 지금의 준비가 얼마나 중요한지를 다시 생각하게 합니다.

이러한 변화 속에서 중요한 것은 협력입니다. AI는 한 국가나 한 기업의 힘만으로 완성할 수 있는 기술이 아니며, 글로벌 연구 역량과 산업 기반이 연결될 때 더 큰 성과를 만들어낼 수 있습니다.

이번 MoU를 통해 과학기술 협력, 인재 양성, AI 안전을 중심으로 협력을 구체화해 나가겠습니다. 특히 AI 안전은 기술 발전과 함께 반드시 함께 가야 할 과제입니다.

알파고 이후 10년이 가능성을 확인한 시간이었다면, 앞으로의 10년은 그 가능성을 현실로 만들어가는 시간이 될 것입니다.

#과기정통부 #AI협력 #K_문샷 #GoogleDeepMind @demishassabis

The Higgs boson didn’t just answer a question, it opened the door to deeper mysteries waiting to be solved.

One of the things I discuss here:

We’re not “launching TikTok shop” as much as we are trying activate creators and affiliates at scale.

TikTok has the best native tools: creator discovery, sampling, product tagging, native affiliate payments, etc

But Meta and YouTube are following fast to give brands the same ability to build a creator-content flywheel.

We were years behind in building an affiliate program with TikTok, but developing one this year will hopefully become more valuable as these creators + content can be leveraged across more platforms.

kept.text:
Good post which reenforces my investment in $TIG.

TIG barely has anything to do with this space. At its core @tigfoundation is an IP licensing and algorithmic discovery protocol that happens to use a token for coordination, the crypto parts are mostly plumbing and not the product.

No one and I mean absolutely no one is talking about algorithms as far as an investment thesis goes because the market for it literally never existed before @tigfoundation. 

But algorithms are also on the tip of the tongue of every AI CEO/CTO because they know compute has limits and the world changing gains come from algos next. 

TIG is comprised of gigabrain academics which realized the best way to keep it open source is through a token rewards mechanism. It doesn't matter which broccoli haired zoomer holding for 1 minute is on or off this token. Once they start licensing algos, "buybacks" will take on a different meaning. 

And this is all the while when TIG has created the first commercial/open source combo to make this all feasible.

The biggest gains in crypto are ALWAYS from a new narrative.

AI is cool but you're going up against trillion dollar companies with infinite funding. Good luck. 

Algorithms are the next step. If you want to feel early in "crypto" again. Look towards algorithms. Look at $TIG.

quoted_tweet.text:
Crypto's current state is a bit shit

1. Market cap is not an indicator of quality - the top 50 is made up of ghost coins or bloated governance slop that has underperformed and is uninvestable 

2. The long tail speculative stuff went from high risk high reward to 'some dude in Miami is going to zero this if you hold it for more than 5.9 seconds'

3. Everything is extremely correlated and you can't meaningfully make bets based on sectors as it all converges into a tightly correlated mush, especially to the downside

4. Broad brush alt season is an artefact of the past that's very hard to replicate given (2) and given that there are simply too many coins and the excess of speculation doesn't really happen on centralised exchanges anymore - it's been siphoned off to bundled shit in max PvP settings

5. Crypto reputationally is no longer the sexy frontier of speculation. Institutional bid is in AI, retail speculative bid is in 0DTE equities, single name stocks etc.

6. Convexity has flattened. Even a lot of the historically safe blue chip stuff (BTC, ETH etc.) has underperformed and the historical anchor of 'buy deep drawdowns because all-time highs are guaranteed and explosive' has disappointed. All the shit we used to put up with because of the accessibly massive trend and momentum effects is now harder to justify because those same effects are getting neutered or siphoned off into other arenas.

The obvious rebuttal is 'cycles' but even this past cycle is a useful counterpoint: it was extremely concentrated versus broad brush wealth effect, plus something very obviously broke after 10/10.

So what does this all mean?

1. In previous cycles, nailing timing was enough and selection was the cherry on top (rising tide lifted all boats). I don't think that holds - both timing and selection matter now and in the future.

2. Participation alone can be an edge if the asset class is early enough and/or mispriced enough. I don't think that holds either, and we might actually have to learn how to trade (fuck).

3. Hopefully I'm an idiot doomposting the bottom

GM

The discovery story of the Jubail Church is considered one of the most interesting coincidences in the world of archaeology:

The church came to light in 1986, entirely by chance in the middle of the desert. It was discovered when a group wandering among the sand dunes (or according to some sources, an off-road vehicle) stumbled upon the structure buried under the sand, or when the walls became visible due to the shifting of the dunes.

Note: The photo is for illustrative purposes.

kept.text:
The "Quadrant Experience" meeting is when the rubber meets the road with discovery

Qualified prospective clients get draft deliverables with real data + my full attention and consulting power for a 1 hour meeting 

Takeaways to include:

-Detailed statement of net worth
-High level financial plan input / cash flow
-Tax report / scenarios / DAF explainer
-Portfolio X-ray / analysis / philosophy
-Observations and onboarding guide
-Consulting for near term decision / liquidity event
-Quoted fee level

Measure twice, cut once.

profile_bio.description: Personal CFO for 7-8 figure tech employees, founders & business owners • Director, Wealth Solutions @ Quadrant Capital • Tweets ≠ Advice • Book an intro call ↓
